description: Trigger discrimination for the error MODEL (this skill: codes, envelope, retryable, disclosure) vs the API CONTRACT that carries it (api-event-architect) vs how the UI RENDERS the error state (edge-state-ux-designer).

cases.prompt: Define our error codes and envelope: the set of error categories, a stable machine-readable code per category, which are retryable, and a rule so we never leak internals in messages.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — a category set, stable codes, retryability, and a disclosure rule are the error model. No routes/versioning/rate-limits and no UI rendering are being designed.
cases.reason: The taxonomy, envelope, retry classification, and message-disclosure discipline are this skill's core deliverable, independent of the transport contract and of presentation.

cases.prompt: Design our public API contract: resource routes, request/response envelopes, version negotiation and deprecation windows, idempotency keys, and per-plan rate limits.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (reverse) — routes, versioning, idempotency, and rate limits are the contract surface. The error model is one part inside it, not what is being designed here.
cases.reason: api-event-architect owns the external contract. This skill designs the error taxonomy that lives inside it and hands code versioning/deprecation to that contract process.

cases.prompt: When these errors happen in the app, decide how to show them — inline field messages vs a toast vs a full-page error, the retry affordance, and the empty vs error distinction on the list view.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (within pack) — inline vs toast vs full-page, retry affordance, and empty-vs-error rendering are UI state design. The codes/messages being rendered come FROM this skill but are not what is asked.
cases.reason: edge-state-ux-designer owns how non-happy-path states are presented. This skill supplies the machine codes and message patterns that rendering consumes.
